title: Bounded measurement for segmentation and outcome-consistency gate
summary: A live bounded snapshot shows that the ungated top regression candidate would still be the research/null-task_type case, while the gated live script emits no regression candidate. This supports the claim that the current gate suppresses thin-evidence escalation, but it is still not a longitudinal end-to-end proof.

baseline_comparator.why_this_baseline: No preserved pre-gate historical candidate stream exists, so the most honest comparator is the same live state.db snapshot viewed through the ungated SQL ranking and through the gated emission path.

ungated_snapshot.interpretation: Without the selection gate, the same thin-evidence research/null-task_type case still ranks as the top regression candidate.

gated_snapshot.observed_output: No regression candidate found for current filters.
gated_snapshot.interpretation: With the live gate path active, no regression candidate is emitted from the same underlying state.

observation_window.scope: current live code against current state.db
observation_window.note: This is a narrow operational check, not a repeated longitudinal window.

threshold.minimum_informative_result: An ungated counterfactual candidate exists and the gated live path suppresses it for the documented gate reasons.
threshold.why_this_threshold: This is the smallest threshold that shows the selected intervention is active and behaviorally relevant without overstating downstream impact.

disconfirmation_rule.rule: The measurement is disconfirmed if the gated live path still emits the research/null-task_type candidate or if the gate reason is unrelated to segmentation/outcome inconsistency.
disconfirmation_rule.stronger_disconfirmation: A later bounded window showing repeated false-positive regression candidates despite the gate would also weaken the intervention claim.

live_gate_checks.output: task_type fehlt; zuerst Segmentierung sichern, nicht Skill bewerten
Outcome-Definition wirkt inkonsistent: sehr niedrige Erfolgsrate (0.0%) bei noch mittlerer/hoeherer Avg Quality (0.5).

observed_effect.primary: The selected case no longer escalates into emitted regression-candidate handling under the live gated path.
observed_effect.secondary: The suppression reason matches the proof-case rationale: missing segmentation first, outcome-definition inconsistency second.

verdict.statement: The bounded live check supports that the current gate prevents the recovered research/null-task_type case from surfacing as a regression candidate. This is evidence for instrumentation-first handling, but not yet a full closed-loop proof of downstream improvement.

limitations: The comparator is counterfactual same-snapshot, not a preserved historical pre/post series.
There is still no repeated observation window for downstream review-quality effects.
The result shows suppression of one thin-evidence case, not a broad causal reduction across all future false positives.
Historical provenance gaps in legacy rows remain unresolved.

next_step_after_measurement.name: Finalize proof-case verdict posture and optionally add a repeated observation window
actions: decide whether this bounded measurement is enough for a local proof-case verdict
if stronger confidence is needed, collect repeated candidate-emission checks over a defined window
document whether downstream review workload quality measurably changes
